column1: SI Deployment Checklist — Organized by HubSpot CRM Sales Pipeline Stages
Combined Internal + External checklist organized by deployment lifecycle. [Internal] = Instrumental team only. [External] = Customer/CM/SI facing. [Both] = Joint responsibility.
Lifecycle: SIRD Drafting > SIRD Approved > DFM (SI) > DFM Approved > Quote Received > Quote Approved > PO Issued > Build > FAT > Shipped > SAT > Warranty/Legal Window > Deployment Complete

column4: Checklist Item
Scope AOI use case with customer and site (product, inspection type, line layout)
Define imaging requirements (camera type, lens, lighting, working distance, resolution)
Define mechanical requirements (fixture dimensions, mounting interfaces, GD&T, CTQs)
Define automation requirements (cycle time target, UPH target, motion control type)
Define performance specs: Target UPH: ___ | Cycle time/unit: ___ | Automation sequence budget: ___ | AI + operator review budget: ___
Include calibration and validation procedures (aperture, focus, gain)
Include 2D drawings (Section 7.3) and provide 3D CAD models of imaging hardware to SI
SIRD version-controlled and stored in shared repository (e.g., Google Drive)
Customer confirms product scope, inspection priorities, and line/site details
Cross-functional review completed: HDE / SA / TPM / Product Mgmt reviewed and approved SIRD
SIRD version number locked: ___
SI acknowledged receipt and confirmed understanding of SIRD
Customer (OEM) acknowledged and approved SIRD scope
SIRD sent to SI with all referenced drawings and CAD files
SIRD templatization status confirmed (reusable for future deployments)
SI DFM package received (mechanical fixture design, automation layout, electrical design)
Mechanical fixture design reviewed: dimensions, tolerances, material selection
Layout drawings reviewed: line integration, space constraints, operator access, conveyor alignment
Automation design reviewed: motion control, conveyors, lifters, gantry systems, tray feeders, rotation platforms
Camera module mounting and positioning validated against SIRD imaging specs
Safety features reviewed: e-stops, light curtains, guards, lockout/tagout provisions
Cable routing and length requirements validated vs. real factory layout
Power and compressed air supply requirements documented
CM/Customer confirms line layout compatibility and installation location dimensions
DFM formally approved by Instrumental (SA + HDE + TPM sign-off)
Any design changes from original SIRD documented via change-order process
Impact on cost, schedule, and performance assessed for any deviations

column1: 4a. Confirm BOM & Hardware Instrumental Needs to Provide

column4: Hardware responsibility confirmed: [ ] SI sourcing per IN spec  OR  [ ] IN consigning to SI

Lenses procured/allocated (type, qty): ___
Lighting procured/allocated (type, qty): ___
Edge AI PC / station computer sized and procured
Monitor(s) procured (incl. 2nd monitor for pass/fail if required)
All cables, adapters, connectors, stacklights, barcode scanners included
BOM finalized: all parts identified as MTO or OTS, long lead items flagged, spares ordered
3D CAD models of all IN-supplied hardware delivered to SI
Station provisioning kicked off per onboarding form (link: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/[FORM_ID])

Project ID / Project Name / Scope documented: ___
Target shipping date for provisioned stations confirmed: ___
Number of stations to provision: ___
Station computer OS and Instrumental software image confirmed
SI quote received covering: design, fabrication, integration, shipping, installation, spare parts, taxes/duties
Quote includes subsystem responsibility split (SI vs. Instrumental vs. site-provided)
Shipping term and destination explicit (Incoterm)
Quote validity period and currency confirmed
Warranty duration and coverage confirmed in quote (e.g., 1yr vs 3yr)
Change-order process and pricing rules included
Quote reviewed against Appendix A minimum fields (per Exec Memo)
Quote pricing confirmed acceptable by Instrumental and customer
Payment milestone schedule agreed (e.g., 60/20/20)
Customer (OEM) budget confirmed
Ownership & title transfer model confirmed (e.g., OEM-owned, CM-consigned)
Warranty start trigger defined (e.g., SAT sign-off date)
SI framework agreement or SOW executed
NDA with SI executed; IP protection / station hardening plan confirmed
Asset tagging and serialization plan agreed
PO issued to SI; SI confirms receipt and kickoff date
SI build schedule received and baselined: BOM > Fabrication > Assembly > Debug > FAT
Milestone dates aligned with deployment target date
Weekly/biweekly build status cadence established with SI
SI material lead times confirmed and tracked (L/T standard parts, electrical, fabrication)
Station provisioning onboarding form completed (link: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/[FORM_ID])
Station type(s): ___
Project ID / Project Name / Scope: ___
Shipping date for provisioned stations to SI or site: ___
Station computer provisioned with OS, Instrumental software, and image stream config
AI models trained and validated for target product; model deployed to edge device
Automated review thresholds set
Customer + CM + SI + Instrumental alignment meeting completed; all stakeholders aware of timeline
Stakeholder matrix documented (Customer POC, CM POC, SI PM, Instrumental TPM/SA/HDE/CSE)
Escalation path defined: L1 CM > L2 SI > L3 Customer > L4 Instrumental > L5 Management
Installation location confirmed on floor plan; area clearing scheduled
Power drops, network drops, compressed air (if needed) confirmed at station location
IT readiness: IP addresses provided, firewall rules configured, ethernet ports tested
MES integration requirements defined (protocol, endpoints, data fields, test environment)

L/T materials purchased by SI
Structure, standard ME parts, electrical parts, fabrication parts purchased
Mechanical assembly complete
Electrical assembly complete
PLC/PC program loaded and I/O check complete
Phase 1: Communication testing with Instrumental (offline) — verify station software connects
Single machine debugging and trial run complete
Whole line trial run complete (if multi-station deployment)
Provisioned station computers shipped to SI (or directly to site)
SI confirms receipt of all Instrumental-supplied hardware (cameras, lenses, lights, PCs)
Hardware integrated into SI-built fixture/cell
Installation location physically cleared; evidence provided (photos)
Floor load capacity verified for automated equipment
Loading dock, forklift, rigging equipment confirmed for receiving
Pathway from dock to install location cleared and measured (door widths, corridors, elevators)
Production line downtime scheduled and confirmed for installation window
Safety review of automated equipment completed by site safety team
PPE requirements communicated; who provides confirmed
Golden units and known defect samples prepared for validation
Operator scheduling: operators identified for training and initial production runs
FAT date confirmed: ___  |  Instrumental personnel attending identified
FAT criteria documented and agreed between Instrumental and SI
[ ] Mechanical: fixture dimensions within tolerance, smooth motion on all axes
[ ] Imaging: camera focus, aperture, gain calibrated; image quality meets SIRD spec
[ ] Automation: cycle time within budget, all motion sequences operational (load/unload, rotation, gantry)
[ ] Software: Instrumental app connects, images upload successfully, health checks pass
[ ] Safety: e-stops functional, guards in place, lockout/tagout verified
[ ] Electrical: all I/O functional, wiring per schematic, power consumption within spec
[ ] Barcode/unit tracking: scanner reads correctly, serial numbers captured
[ ] Data: images transfer at required resolution and speed
FAT Result: [ ] PASS  [ ] CONDITIONAL PASS (punch list with due dates)  [ ] FAIL (re-test date agreed)
FAT sign-off: Instrumental SA: ___  Date: ___  |  SI QA: ___  Date: ___
If Conditional Pass: improvement items tracked and verified before shipment
SI responsible for packing per agreed standards; fragile components secured (cameras, lenses, lights)
Anti-static packaging for electronic components confirmed
Shipping method confirmed: [ ] Sea  [ ] Air  [ ] Ground  |  Incoterm: ___
Transit time aligned with deployment schedule; ETA at site: ___
Insurance and risk of loss during transit assigned per SOW
Customs documentation prepared (commercial invoice, packing list, COO if applicable)
Receiving contact and location confirmed at destination site
Equipment storage location designated and secured
Unloading equipment available (forklift, pallet jack, crane if needed)
SI installation team onsite; Instrumental deployment team onsite
Equipment moved from storage to installation location; rigging complete
Station installed and leveled at final production location
All connections verified: power, network, compressed air, cameras, PLC, MES
Instrumental software operational and communicating; health checks pass
Image stream and data stream configuration verified in production environment
[ ] Golden unit validation passed
[ ] Known defect sample detection verified
[ ] Cycle time meets target UPH in production environment
[ ] Automation sequences operational (load/unload, tray feed, rotation) in production
[ ] MES integration verified (data flowing to/from line systems)
[ ] Barcode scanning and serial number capture working
[ ] Operator training completed
[ ] 24hr/48hr production run completed without critical failures
SAT Result: [ ] PASS  [ ] CONDITIONAL PASS (punch list)  [ ] FAIL (remediation plan)
SAT sign-off: IN TPM: ___ | SI: ___ | CM/Customer: ___  Date: ___
If PASS: Warranty clock starts. Station officially enters production.
Warranty window start date: ___ (= SAT pass date)  |  Window end date: ___
Monitoring period: station performance tracked daily during warranty window
Key metrics monitored: uptime %, UPH achieved, false alarm rate, image quality consistency
Any defects or failures during window documented with root cause and resolution
SI onsite support active: response time SLA enforced (e.g., onsite within 1 day)
Instrumental software/imaging escalation path active and tested
Spare parts inventory confirmed on-site or regionally by SI
If critical issues arise: remediation plan agreed; warranty window may be extended
Final payment milestone hold: last payment (e.g., 20%) held until warranty window closes cleanly
Legal review: confirm all contractual obligations met before closing deployment
Warranty window closed with no outstanding critical issues
Final payment milestone released to SI
Deployment formally accepted by all parties (Instrumental, SI, Customer, CM)
Deployment serial number and asset registered in deployment portal / webapp
Warranty start date, coverage scope, and SI SLA terms recorded in system of record
Approved drawing/spec revision logged in asset registry
CM maintenance personnel trained on daily and preventative maintenance
Troubleshooting guide provided to CM
Escalation contacts documented: L1 CM POC > L2 SI POC > L3 Instrumental
Quality data review cadence established (weekly/monthly)
First article review completed; FPY, false alarm rate, process improvement metrics baselined
Lessons learned documented; feedback incorporated into SIRD template for future deployments
SI scorecard updated: lead time, install quality, FAT first-pass rate, documentation quality
"Instrumental Inside" branding/sticker applied to deployed station (if applicable)
Station hardening and source code protection verified post-deployment
